observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to create a special account in the general fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities through an ordinance by the local sanggunian, for easy monitoring/ tracking of the utilization of the grant/fund, pursuant to Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular No. 125.
observations.managementAction:
For CY 2021, the Management opted not to create a special account for BGCM and did not charged any expenses for the remaining balance. 
In February 2022, the Management returned the amount of ₱45,719.77 to the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: Management did not create a special account for BGCM.

observations.recommendation: We have  recommended and Management agreed to ensure the timely submission of the advance copies of the perfected contracts together with its supporting documents within five working days after the execution thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management attached the documents upon payment of the contract.
observations.reason: Advance copies of the perfected contracts for CY 2021 were not submitted to the COA office within five working days from the execution of the contract agreements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to deduct the liquidated damages from any money due or which may become due to the contractor or collect such liquidated damages from other securities posted by the contractor, pursuant to regulations.
observations.managementAction: The contractor can no longer be located.
observations.reason: The Management did not issue any collection notice to the contractor for CY 2021. Thus, no liquidated damages were collected.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, in the submission of contracts including their supporting documents in order to implement a systematic and effective review process with a view of generating timely and; relevant audit results and to avoid administrative disciplinary action as provided in Section 127 of P.D. No. 1445.

We have recommended and Management agreed to revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund the unused accumulated engineering overhead of completed and paid infrastructure projects of the Municipality for appropriation to other priority programs, projects and activities. Likewise, for Management to remit back the unused accumulated engineering overhead of the 11 barangays to be used for their other priority projects.

We have recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, ensure that payment of RATA to the aforementioned elective officials shall be supported with complete documentation including work schedule and actual work attendance in accordance with the provisions of DBM LBC No. 103 and P.D. No. 1445, otherwise, payments made will be disallowed in audit.

We have recommended and Management agreed to require the contractors concerned to immediately carry out the necessary corrective measures to address the noted defects to prevent further deterioration and early destruction of the project; and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended Management to compute and collect/deduct the corresponding liquidated damages from any money due to the contractors pursuant to the provisions of Item 8, Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended and the Local Chief Executive as well as the members of the Sanggunian Bayan agreed that, henceforth, ensure that any realignment from the savings of the approved budget shall be made thru an appropriation ordinance covering supplemental budget and not by way of SB resolution in accordance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and the Budget Operations Manual.

We have recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, recognize liability at the time goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received pursuant to regulations.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to periodically reconcile their Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and Registries of the 5% LFRRMF to provide reliable information to stakeholders.

We have recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, ensure that the BAC, its Secretariat and TWG members, attend procurement trainings or capacity development programs and should have satisfactorily completed such trainings or programs conducted, authorized or accredited by the GPPB through its Technical Support Office, within six months upon their designation, pursuant to the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184, updated as of March 31, 2021.

We have recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, ensure to abide with the terms and conditions as stipulated in the MOA, such as the submission of the required reports to the source agency and to the Commission on Audit for the timely review and evaluation thereof.

We have recommended and Management agreed to fast track the implementation of the 20% Development Fund projects/programs/activities, to achieve the purpose for which the fund was appropriated and in order for the stakeholders to enjoy the benefits that could be derived therefrom.
We have recommended further and Management agreed to evaluate the unimplemented PPAs whether these are still priority concerns or had become irrelevant and to re-appropriate the funds to current priority undertakings.

We have recommended and Management agreed to strengthen its fiscal management and properly implement and utilize funds granted to the Municipality in order for the stakeholders to enjoy the optimum benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We have recommended and Management agreed to create a Gender and Development Monitoring and Evaluation Team which shall be composed of the GFPS TWG Chair, Municipal Planning and Development coordinator, HRM Officer, Budget Officer, DILG LGOO, representative/s of the LCE, CSO representative/s specifically from women’s organization and academe in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, to strengthen their monitoring and evaluation system of the Magna Carta of Women.

We have recommended and Management agreed:

to settle the audit suspensions within 90 days after receipt thereof to avoid suspensions maturing into disallowances; and

notify in writing all persons identified to be liable for audit disallowances, request for the settlement of their obligations and deduct from whatever amount due them.

We have reiterated our recommendations in the previous year and Management agreed to require the officials concerned to conduct inventory and prepare the required Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the Municipality; and for the Municipal Accountant to disclose the details of the Road Networks account using the pro-forma disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements provided in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction:
Inventory of road networks to be conducted.

The Municipal Accountant disclosed the details of the Road Network in the CY 2021 Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2021, the Inventory Committee created under EO No. 33-2021, has not yet conducted any inventory taking for the road networks under the jurisdiction of LGU Sagada which resulted in the non-preparation of the required inventory report. However, this was disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team for verification; and henceforth, ensure that all documentary requirements for emergency purchase under R.A. No. 11469 as well as the provision of Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 are complied with before certifying as to completeness of the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers prior to payment, to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the lacking documents and verified by the Audit Team to be in order.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management submit immediately the abovementioned documents to the audit team to ensure the validity and propriety of the amount claimed, and to avoid disallowance.

We have recommended further that, henceforth, Management ensure that all claims against governments funds must be supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the lacking documents and verified by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management strictly pay only the number of days the officials have physically reported for work during the Enhanced Community Quarantine period in accordance with Item 4.4 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-1; and to refund the excess payment made to the officials/employees concerned.
observations.managementAction: Excess payment were being collected from the responsible officials/employees.
observations.reason: A balance of ₱50,250.00 has not yet been paid by the concerned officials/ employees.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, utilize the 30 percent Quick Response Fund only if supported with a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and upon declaration of the State of Calamity; and to use the same for relief and recovery programs only pursuant to the provisions of COA- Circular No. 2012-002 and R.A. No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: No utilization for 30% QRF for CY 2021. Disbursements of lumpsum appropriations of other funds were supported with SB resolutions.
observations.reason:
Implementation of the recommendation cannot be determined since no utilization made on the 30% QRF for CY 2021.
Utilization of other programs funded under the lump sum appropriation 20% MDF were supported by a resolution.

observations.recommendation: We have   recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, utilize the fund exclusively for the purpose for which they have been appropriated, any changes thereof may be made through augmentation pursuant to the provisions of R.A No. 7160 and SB Appropriation Ordinance No. 03-2020, to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Agency utilized the TF-LDRRMF based on the approved LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to:
henceforth, acknowledge all cash donations through the issuance of an Officer Receipt, and deposit the same in the accounts of the Municipality with an authorized government depository bank; and 

ensure that all disbursements shall be made by the checks drawn against the depository account of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Cash donations received by the Agency for CY 2021 were acknowledged through the issuance of an official receipt.



Disbursements charged to donations were issued through checks drawn against the depository account of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, ensure the preparation of the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and submit the same within ten  working days after the end of each quarter to the NDRRMC, through the OCD and copy furnished the COA Auditors in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO submitted a report to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: For CY 2021, the Management submitted the report to the Audit Team once only. Also, they did not submit the report to the OCD.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management submit SB Resolutions and approval of the chief executive authorizing the deposit of the amounts of ₱2,000,000.00 and ₱22,633,371.82 in Time Deposit and HYSA, respectively. Also, submit proofs that the amounts deposited are idle funds, otherwise, terminate these accounts and transfer the principal amounts and accumulated interest of ₱450,683.97 and ₱1,034,561.00, respectively, to the Cash in Bank-LCCA in the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management terminated the accounts and transferred the principal and accumulated interest to the Cash in Bank-LCCA General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We have  recommended and Management agreed to transfer its Cash in Bank account from the Philippine National Bank to the Land Bank of the Philippines in accordance with Items 5.2 and 6.1 of the Department of Finance Department Circular Nos. 001.2015 and 003.2015, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management transferred the amount to the LBP GF account and terminated the time deposit account.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the BAC through the BAC Secretariat agreed to henceforth, prepare an Annual Procurement Plan pursuant to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and Manual on Establishing Procurement Systems and Organizations for LGUs.

We have further recommended further  and Management agreed to prepare the Annual Procurement Plan in accordance with the prescribed format (Annex A) of GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16, 2015.
observations.managementAction:
Management prepared the APP in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184.



The APP was prepared in accordance with the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the BAC Secretariat agreed to, henceforth, submit the electronic Annual Procurement Plan and the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB pursuant to the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 02-2020.
observations.managementAction: The APP for CY 2021 and 1st semester PMR were submitted to GPPB in accordance w/ GPPB Circular No. 02-2020.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to recognize the cost of completed development projects into its proper asset account by preparing an adjusting journal entry to correct the balance of the affected accounts for the fair presentation in the financial statements.

We have recommended further and Management agreed to henceforth, recognize the cost of development projects funded from the 20% Development Fund in the appropriate asset account in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and Section 50, Chapter 3 of the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
Management made the necessary adjustments to correct the balance of the affected accounts.



Projects paid in CY 2021 under the 20% Development Fund were recorded in its appropriate asset account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to conduct an annual inventory taking for all the property owned/controlled by the Municipality; and to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property and Equipment in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: The Management started the inventory taking per office.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2021, Management was not able to finish its physical inventory of PPE for CY 2021. Consequently, they were not able to prepare the report on the physical count of property and equipment.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to require the Property Officer to review the inventory list of equipment and machineries that are issued to employees; renew the AREs every three years if there is no change in property custodianship; and issue an ARE every time accountability of government property is transferred. Moreover, ARE must be properly filled up with the needed data in accordance with the Manual on Property and Supply Management System.
observations.managementAction: Property and Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) for properties previously issued to separated employees was updated and transferred to other employees.
observations.reason: Due to the unfinished physical inventory of all PPE, Management was not able to update all the PAR of its PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the members of the GAD Focal Point System agreed to prepare and submit immediately to the Auditor the GAD Accomplishment Report for Calendar Year 2020, following the prescribed format pursuant to Items 4.1.A.4.g and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW, DBM, DILG and NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We  have  recommended further that the GAD AR as prescribed in Joint Memorandum Circular be accompanied by the following: (1) brief summary of the reported program or project; (2) copies of reported policy issuances; (3) results of HGDG tests; and (4) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations, if any.
observations.managementAction:
Management submitted its GAD Accomplishment Report.



Supporting documents of the GAD AR of CY 2020 were submitted to the COA office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, require business owners and organizations to submit certified photocopies of their sworn statement of gross sales or receipts for the preceding year and subsequently validate these statements pursuant to regulations.
observations.managementAction: Based on the application forms for the renewal of business permit, the business owners submitted its certified sworn statement of gross sales or receipts for the preceding year.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Collectors agreed to accomplish the Report of Collections and Deposits and turn over their cash collections to the Liquidating Officer at the close of each business day pursuant to the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Reports of Collections and Deposits were accomplished and cash collections were turned over to the Liquidating Officer at the close of each business day or on the next banking day.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to henceforth, ensure the timely submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF were being submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to fully utilize the aforementioned pieces of equipment in order for efficient reduction of residual wastes.
observations.managementAction: The needed spare parts and repair of MRF equipment were provided by the Management. As of December 31, 2021, all equipment at the MRF are in good condition and are being fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and Management agreed to revisit its ordinance to provide specifics on the coverage of the term “tourist”; and
observations.managementAction: Amendment/Revision of the Tourism Code of LGU Sagada is on-going which includes the definition of “tourist”. The Office of the SB committed to pass the resolution adopting the tourism code within the 1st semester of CY 2022.
observations.reason: No amendment in the Revenue Code as of validation date.
